statements.sql: INSERT INTO silver.CorrelatedSubqueryTarget (ProductAlternateKey, EnglishProductName) SELECT CAST(p.ProductID AS NVARCHAR(25)), p.ProductName FROM bronze.Product AS p WHERE p.ProductID = (SELECT MAX(p2.ProductID) FROM bronze.Product AS p2 WHERE p2.ProductName = p.ProductName)

refactor.extracted_sql: SELECT CAST(p.ProductID AS NVARCHAR(25)), p.ProductName FROM bronze.Product AS p WHERE p.ProductID = ( SELECT MAX(p2.ProductID) FROM bronze.Product AS p2 WHERE p2.ProductName = p.ProductName )
refactor.refactored_sql: WITH import AS ( SELECT p.ProductID, p.ProductName FROM bronze.Product AS p ), logical AS ( SELECT i.ProductID, i.ProductName, MAX(i.ProductID) OVER (PARTITION BY i.ProductName) AS max_product_id FROM import AS i ), final AS ( SELECT CAST(l.ProductID AS NVARCHAR(25)) AS ProductID, l.ProductName FROM logical AS l WHERE l.ProductID = l.max_product_id ) SELECT * FROM final

source_tables.summary: Both extracted and refactored SQL query the same source table: bronze.Product

output_columns.summary: Both return the same two columns: ProductID cast to NVARCHAR(25) and ProductName

joins.summary: Neither query uses joins; both operate on a single table (bronze.Product)

filters.summary: Both filter to rows where ProductID equals the maximum ProductID for each ProductName group. Extracted uses correlated subquery; refactored uses window function MAX() OVER (PARTITION BY). Both semantically equivalent.

aggregation_grain.summary: Both return one row per ProductName group with the maximum ProductID value, producing identical result sets
